meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to build your portfolio. By leveraging a production-grade SaaS starter kit paired with Antigravity and Claude Code, you can deploy a full-stack, authenticated application in hours. This strategy allows you to showcase technical depth, database architecture, and UI polish to potential clients immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the technical talent, but without a portfolio, you are invisible to high-paying clients who demand proof of competency before signing a contract. The 'no portfolio' trap isn't a lack of skill; it's a lack of leverage.

To break the cycle, you don't need to spend months on a side project. You need to ship a high-fidelity SaaS starter kit that demonstrates architectural maturity. By utilizing AI-augmented workflows, you can compress a two-week development cycle into a single weekend, creating a showcase piece that proves you can build, scale, and deliver.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation using a modern stack (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind). This ensures your portfolio piece looks like it was built by a senior engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS architecture blueprint using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Tailwind CSS. Include a folder structure for modular components and API route handling.

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
steps.description: Showcase enterprise-grade security by implementing RBAC, a feature most clients require but few junior developers know how to architect.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function and database schema for role-based access control (admin, user, guest). Ensure the UI conditionally renders based on the user session.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement rbac middleware and update db schema for user roles'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Subscription Logic
steps.description: Nothing screams 'real-world experience' like payment integration. This proves you can handle sensitive financial logic safely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler and checkout session flow for recurring monthly subscriptions. Include logic for handling successful payments and updating the user subscription status in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate stripe webhook handler and subscription logic'

steps.title: Develop the Dashboard UI
steps.description: Focus on data visualization and clean UX to prove you understand the value of a user-centric interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-fidelity dashboard layout using shadcn/ui components. Include a dynamic chart for tracking mock user metrics and a clean data table for resource management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build responsive dashboard with shadcn components and mock data metrics'

steps.title: Automate Deployment and CI/CD
steps.description: Professionalism is defined by your deployment pipeline. Automate your testing and deployment to show you understand DevOps principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel. Ensure it runs linting and build checks on every push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup github actions for continuous deployment to vercel'

pro_tips: Never build from scratch; always fork a battle-tested repository and customize the business logic to suit your specific portfolio narrative.
Document your 'why' in the README. Clients care more about your decision-making process than the code itself.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your most complex functions; it turns a 'code demo' into a 'production-ready' case study.
Add a 'Changelog' to your portfolio site. It shows you know how to iterate and maintain software over time.
Record a 60-second Loom video walking through the code architecture. It validates that you actually built it and builds immediate trust.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, push your code to GitHub, add a detailed README explaining the business problem you solved, and link the live demo in your LinkedIn profile or proposal emails.
content.conversion_block: Stop lurking and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day of lost potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
